channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: L’AI ha cambiato tutto: ecco perché per i CIO è la tecnologia del decennio
item.description: Nel 2024 le aziende italiane hanno continuato a investire sul digitale e il trend si confermerà nel 2025. Due le grandi priorità: cybersicurezza e intelligenza artificiale.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: I tried out 3 media outlets’ AI chatbots. Here’s what happened
item.description: How fitting for Time to debut an AI chatbot last week with its annual Person of the Year package. AI was almost certainly Thing of the Year, after all, even if there’s no official award for that.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Looking for small drones? Listen for the right radio signals
item.description: The recent spate of unidentified drone sightings in the U.S., including some near sensitive locations such as airports and military installations, has caused significant public concern. Some of this recent increase in activity may be related to a September 2023 change in U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Who’s who in Donald Trump’s new Silicon Valley entourage
item.description: There’s a larger coterie of tech world Trump supporters who are now working with the incoming administration, with varying degrees of formality.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: La colaboración entre Seguridad y FinOps puede generar beneficios ocultos en la nube
item.description: Para las empresas que operan en la nube, la seguridad y la gestión de costes son preocupaciones crecientes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why Big Tech is funding Trump's inauguration
item.description: Hefty donations from tech companies and leaders to President-elect Trump’s inaugural fund are a sign of Silicon Valley’s shifting relationship with the incoming leader after past tensions.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Albania bans TikTok for a year following death of teenager
item.description: Albania’s prime minister said the country will be banning TikTok for one year, citing violence and bullying among children after a teenager died.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: New leak says if your iPhone can run iOS 18, it should be able to run iOS 19 too
item.description: We have some hints about which iPhones and iPads are going to be able to run the software Apple unveils next year.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Food tracking just got lazy — in the best way possible — with this wearable
item.description: Counting calories just got easier with The Drop, the world's first automated nutrition tracker using advanced computer vision and artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republicans brush aside Musk's primary threats
item.description: House Republicans are largely brushing aside Elon Musk’s threats to unseat members who supported the initial government funding measure he helped torpedo earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — Albania's prime minister said Saturday the government will shut down the video service TikTok for one year, blaming it for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Democrat accuses Musk of tanking spending bill over China restrictions
item.description: The top Democrat on the House Appropriations Committee accused Elon Musk of tanking a bipartisan spending bill because it included a provision that could limit his businesses' ability to operate in China. In a Friday letter to congressional leaders, Rep. Rosa DeLauro (D-Conn.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, The Hill

item.title: GAO report says DHS, other agencies need to up their game in AI risk assessment
item.description: A new report from the US Government Accountability Office (GAO) appears to indicate that no US federal agency reporting into the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) knows the full extent or probability of harm that AI can do the nation’s critical infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Federal News Network’s Industry Exchange Cyber 2025
item.description: How are you preparing to defend against tomorrow’s threats today? The post Federal News Network’s Industry Exchange Cyber 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: What to do if someone is shading you online
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Arm awaits verdict in high-stakes licensing suit against Qualcomm
item.description: Arm is waiting to see if it has won a battle over licensing of its microprocessor designs to Qualcomm that has cast doubt over the immediate future of some of the chip maker’s products.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Scholz dismisses Musk's assertion that only a far-right party can 'save' Germany
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz on Friday dismissed an assertion by Elon Musk that only a far-right party can “save Germany,” but said that freedom of opinion "also goes for multibillionaires.” Germany is expected to vote in an early election on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon workers strike at multiple facilities as Teamsters seek labor contract
item.description: Workers at seven Amazon facilities went on strike Thursday, an effort by the Teamsters union to pressure the e-commerce company for a labor agreement during a key shopping period.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Australian who falsely claimed to have invented bitcoin is found in contempt of UK court
item.description: LONDON (AP) — An Australian computer scientist who falsely claimed to be the founder of the bitcoin cryptocurrency was found Thursday to be in contempt of an order of London's High Court and was sentenced to 12 months in prison, suspended for two years.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk considers funding Nigel Farage's populist party, a move that could shake up UK politics
item.description: LONDON (AP) — It’s a photo that sent a tremor through British politics: Elon Musk flanked by British politician Nigel Farage and a wealthy backer, in front of a gilt-framed painting of a young Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Massive data breach at federal credit union exposes 240,000 members
item.description: A credit union with over 240,000 members recently revealed it was targeted by cybercriminals, resulting in a data breach that was part of a two-month attack by hackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple complains Meta requests risk privacy in spat over EU efforts to widen access to iPhone tech
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple complained that requests from Meta Platforms for access to its operating software threaten user privacy, in a spat fueled by the European Union's intensifying efforts to get the iPhone maker to open up to products from tech rivals.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FAQ: Getting started with Bluesky
item.description: What is Bluesky? Bluesky is a “microblogging” social media network similar to X (formerly Twitter), where people can publish posts with a maximum of 300 characters, as well as follow others, create lists, like posts, and so on.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Getting started with Azure Managed Redis
item.description: Microsoft has made a lot of big bets in its preferred cloud-native infrastructure. You only need to look at .
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US moves to tighten restrictions on China Telecom amid security fears
item.description: The US Commerce Department is intensifying its actions against China Telecom’s US unit over concerns that its cloud and internet services could be used to funnel American data to Beijing, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Smarter devops: How to avoid deployment horrors
item.description: Many devops organizations implement advanced CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and other automations to increase deployment frequency. In the State of DevOps Report 2023, 18% of respondents were classified as elite performers for being able to deploy on-demand with change lead times of less than a day.
item.dc:creator: Info World
